data.title: Intercontinental Exchange reports 23% higher Q1 average daily volume
data.description:
• Intercontinental Exchange (NYSE:ICE) announced on Thursday a 23% year-over-year rise in the first-quarter average daily volume.
  • Energy ADV increased by 24% from last year. Oil ADV was up 18%, natural Gas ADV 33%, and Environmentals ADV 15%.
  • Sugar ADV rose by 20% on a yearly basis.
  • Financials ADV increased by 28%, NYSE Cash Equities ADV by 20%, and NYSE Equity Options ADV by 8%.
  • "The support from our customers has created the depth of liquidity that our markets offer, and we thank them for making the first quarter of 2025 the highest traded volume quarter in ICE’s history," said President Ben Jackson.

data.title: Mitsubishi may consider investing in Alaska LNG project, CEO says
data.description:
Mitsubishi (OTCPK:MSBHF) may consider investing in a liquefied natural gas project in Alaska, although any decision will require careful review, CEO Katsuya Nakanishi said Thursday while unveiling plans to spend at least ¥4T (~$27B) over the next three years.

"We have been approached and are considering it," Nakanishi told reporters when asked about possible participation in the Alaska LNG project.

Mitsubishi (OTCPK:MSBHF) will evaluate the project's feasibility, considering factors such as the 800-mile distance to the U.S. west coast, the need to build liquefaction facilities, and future LNG demand in Southeast Asia and other regions.

Alaskan officials have said the project could attract U.S. investors for most of the pipeline part, while any equity from Asian buyers likely would be tied to offtake from the liquefaction plant that will prepare the gas for export.

Glenfarne recently agreed to acquire a 75% ownership stake in the potential project, according to the Alaska Gasline Development Corporation, the state agency that now holds a 25% stake through its 8 Star Alaska subsidiary.

data.title: Smith-Midland awarded $5M SlenderWall contract for FBI project
data.description:
• Smith-Midland (NASDAQ:SMID) on Thursday said it has been selected to produce more than $5M of SlenderWall and architectural precast concrete panels for existing dormitories at the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) in Quantico, Virginia.
  • Production of SlenderWall, Smith-Midland's high-performance, lightweight precast concrete cladding system, is set to begin in June 2025 at the company's Virginia plant, with installation slated for October 2025.
  • SMID -4.09% premarket to $30.0.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: CrowdStrike appoints chief technology innovation officer
data.description:
• Cybersecurity company CrowdStrike (NASDAQ:CRWD) (NEOE:CRWD:CA) announced on Thursday the appointment of Alex Ionescu as chief technology innovation officer.
  • Shares were -4.51% pre-market at $354.75.
  • The company's founding chief architect and former vice president of endpoint engineering has rejoined in the current role.

data.title: Quant Comparison: Mag7 scores high on profitability, valuations remain a concern
data.description:
The Magnificent 7, which represents about a third of the benchmark S&P 500, has taken a beating this year. 

Sentiments around the growth stocks turned sour even before President Trump launched a trade war, stoking macroeconomic uncertainty and raising recession risks in the U.S, but tariff actions have sent the group even lower.

The collective also lost a whopping $15T in market cap after the launch of China’s DeepSeek’s (DEEPSEEK) R1 open-source AI model back in January, while the Mag7 also accounts for nearly the entire downturn in the Nasdaq 100 YTD. 

The group now trades near levels just prior to the 2020 presidential election, surrendering most of the "animal spirit" gains.

As near-term risk remains apparent for the Magnificent Seven, Seeking Alpha's Quant Ratings points out the visible strengths of this group. 

On the profitability front, SA Quant gave the highest possible grades to the group as a whole. Meanwhile, when considering growth prospects stocks like Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) and Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) outperformed others like Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) and Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL). 

Valuations, however, proved to be a bane for the group. SA Quant gave most stocks the lowest possible rating, while Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) and Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) fared better. 

Mag7 also showed strong momentum, with most stocks performing well on this front. However, Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) and Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG) were the exceptions, scoring a C, the lowest among the bunch. 

Coming to EPS revisions, Mag7 saw varied grades. Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) earned the highest score amongst the bunch, while Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) got the lowest grade among the bunch at D+. 

Seeking Alpha grades each stock by five “factors” -- Value, Growth, Profitability, Momentum, and EPS Revisions. For more details, see Quant Ranking and Factor Grades FAQ.

data.title: Papa Johns and Google Cloud team up to deliver AI-powered pizza experiences
data.description:
• Google Cloud (NASDAQ:GOOG) Thursday reported an expanded multi-year partnership with Papa Johns (NASDAQ:PZZA) to transform the pizza maker's ordering and delivery experience using artificial intelligence.
  • Through the partnership, Papa Johns has created an innovation team, named PJX, that will leverage Google Cloud's (AI), data analytics and machine learning capabilities.
  • Papa Johns will also be able to predict customer ordering patterns to provide relevant promotions and ordering shortcuts.

data.title: Initial jobless claims unexpectedly fall in past week
data.description:
Initial jobless claims for week ended March 29: -6K to 219K vs. 226K consensus and 225K prior (revised from 224K), according to data released by the U.S. Department of Labor on Thursday.

Continuing claims: 1.903M vs. 1.860M consensus and 1.847M prior (revised from 1.856M).

Developing... check back for updates

data.title: U.S. trade in goods deficit narrows slightly less than expected in February
data.description: The U.S.'s international trade in goods deficit to -$122.7B in February, compared with the -$122.5B expected and -$130.7B prior (revised from -$131.4B), according to data released by the U.S. Commerce Department on Friday.

data.title: Enphase Energy adds battery backup to boost resilience in France and the Netherlands
data.description:
• Enphase Energy (NASDAQ:ENPH) Thursday announced the introduction of the IQ System Controller in France and the Netherlands.
  • The system integrates Enphase's IQ Microinverters and IQ Battery 5Ps to provide comprehensive energy solution that enables homeowners to seamlessly transition to backup power during grid outages.
  • "Our expansion into the European market with the IQ System Controller is another important milestone for Enphase as we continue to grow our footprint across the globe," said Sabbas Daniel, senior vice president of sales at Enphase Energy (NASDAQ:ENPH).

data.title: Newegg's reverse stock split to take effect from April 7
data.description:
• Newegg Commerce (NASDAQ:NEGG) on Thursday said that its twenty-to-one share combination of its common shares will become effective on April 7, 2025.
  • This will result in a proportionate increase to the par value of the company’s common shares from $0.021848 per share to $0.43696 per share.
  • The share combination is intended to enable the company to continue to meet minimum Nasdaq listing price per share requirements and will affect all shareholders proportionately.
  • NEGG -1.33% premarket to $0.266.
  • Source: Press Release
